name: for-the-love-of-physics
description: Walter Lewin's For the Love of Physics — a physics appreciation toolkit from the MIT professor whose passionate lectures made physics accessible to millions, covering Newton's laws, electromagnetism, optics, astronomy, and the beauty of understanding the physical world.
useCase: See the wonder in physics — understand rainbows, pendulums, electricity, and the universe through the eyes of a passionate teacher.
knownRisks: In 2014, MIT found Lewin had sexually harassed a student — the book (2011) predates this. The physics teaching remains valuable; the author's misconduct should be acknowledged
The book is designed for general readers, not physics students — it prioritizes wonder over rigor
Some demonstrations described may be dangerous to replicate at home
